meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve no version control (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Solve Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn to master API integration and version control using Antigravity and Claude Code to speed up your freelance development workflow today.
content.answer_block: To eliminate the chaos of unversioned API integrations, implement a Git-based workflow orchestrated by Claude Code. By treating API configurations as code and using Antigravity to abstract endpoint management, you create a robust, auditable history that allows for instant rollbacks and seamless client handoffs, ensuring professional-grade reliability every time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a production environment that feels like a house of cards. A client needs an urgent fix, but your local setup is a graveyard of 'final_v2_fix' folders, and your API integrations are hardcoded strings scattered across three different files. The anxiety of potentially breaking a live endpoint without a safety net is the silent killer of your freelance reputation.

It doesn't have to be this way. By shifting your workflow to a version-controlled, automated integration model, you transform from a reactive coder into a high-velocity architect. We’re going to use Antigravity to structure your API layer and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring that every integration is documented, tested, and perfectly synced.

steps.title: Initialize Versioned API Layer
steps.description: Establish a dedicated directory structure for API endpoints to ensure they are tracked by Git from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized API client structure with separate files for configuration, interceptors, and endpoint definitions.

steps.title: Abstract Endpoints with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to decouple your business logic from raw fetch calls, making your code easier to version and refactor.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert these raw fetch requests into an Antigravity-managed service layer with TypeScript interfaces.

steps.title: Automate Versioning with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce commit standards for every API change, ensuring your history reflects actual integration logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a git hook that validates API schema changes before allowing a commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: update user-auth endpoint schema'

steps.title: Implement Automated Health Checks
steps.description: Create a self-testing suite that runs on every commit to ensure your API integration remains compatible with the backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests that verify API response schemas against the current configuration.

steps.title: Client Handoff Serialization
steps.description: Package your API layer into a clean, documented export that allows clients to maintain the work without needing your local environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md that documents all API endpoints and provides instructions for environment variable setup.

pro_tips: Always store API keys in .env.example files, never in the codebase; use Antigravity to inject them at runtime.
Use git-tagging to version your API integrations (e.g., v1.0.0-api-stable) so you can roll back to a known-working state instantly.
Treat your API documentation as code; let Claude Code update your Swagger/OpenAPI specs automatically when endpoints change.
Implement request logging in your dev environment that pipes to a local file, ensuring you have a paper trail of every failed call.
Use branch-based testing to verify API updates against a staging environment before merging into the main production branch.
resources: Git Best Practices for APIs: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Review your git history to ensure all API changes are properly documented, then set up a CI/CD pipeline to automate deployments.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with broken code and start shipping like a senior engineer. Our community provides the templates, logic, and support you need to scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
